cells.value: ** spell out zero–nine; numerals for 10+. **Exceptions — always numerals: percentages, ages, ratios.** Percentages use the % sign (e.g., 74%).

** when citing clinical data, avoid negative or fear-based language; use an empathetic, supportive, trust-building tone without oversimplifying. Example pattern: "Participants in the VALOR study taking LISRAYA had nearly 50% more improvement in their DM symptoms after one year compared to placebo."
** Oxford/serial comma always in series of 3+.
